question_text: Two waves are created in a rope. Wave A has an amplitude of 10 cm and a frequency of 2 Hz. Wave B has an amplitude of 5 cm and a frequency of 4 Hz. Which statement correctly compares the energy carried by these waves?

options.label: A
options.text: Wave A carries more energy because it has a larger amplitude

options.label: B
options.text: Wave B carries more energy because it has a higher frequency

options.label: C
options.text: Both waves carry equal amounts of energy

options.label: D
options.text: Energy cannot be compared without knowing the wavelength

correct_answer: Wave A carries more energy because it has a larger amplitude
explanation: The energy carried by a wave depends on its amplitude squared. Wave A has an amplitude of 10 cm (twice that of Wave B's 5 cm), so it carries 4 times more energy. Although Wave B has a higher frequency, amplitude has a stronger effect on energy than frequency does. Therefore, Wave A carries significantly more energy overall.
